description: Round 2 runtime hardening: connect the recovery state machine to the live runtime path, complete context governance contracts, unify execution trace payloads for console consumers, and add a durable regression harness before moving into broader platform governance.

userStories.title: Wire RecoveryStateMachine into Runtime Flow
userStories.description: As a runtime maintainer, I want the main recovery path to be driven by RecoveryStateMachine so retry, skip, partial replan, and checkpoint replan use one explicit source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: runtime_agent integrates RecoveryStateMachine into the main recovery flow instead of leaving it as a detached helper
checkpoint updates, recovery attempts, and replan event writes stay behaviorally compatible while becoming more explicit
Skip-step, rebuild-remaining-plan, and full replan paths still work under the unified state flow
Add or update focused backend tests for runtime recovery transitions
Run the matching backend unittest modules

userStories.title: Complete Context Governance Contract
userStories.description: As a runtime maintainer, I want ContextLayer and ContextBudgetUsage to expose complete budget, trim, and compression metadata for the major context sources so multi-turn execution is easier to reason about.
acceptanceCriteria: Context governance metadata covers the major runtime layers including recalled memories, profile hits, and session-derived context
Runtime updates after recall or compression keep the structured budget and trim fields accurate
Current UI payloads remain compatible while gaining richer context trace metadata
Add or update backend tests for context governance and budget updates
Run the matching backend unittest modules

userStories.title: Unify Execution Trace Contract for Console Consumers
userStories.description: As a product maintainer, I want execution, recovery, context, and tool traces to align on a clearer contract so the console and task detail views can rely less on UI-side inference.
acceptanceCriteria: Execution trace payloads expose stable kinds, labels, and metadata for recovery, context, and tool-related events
ExecutionRegistry definitions are used more consistently by runtime trace producers
Current console and task-detail consumers remain compatible or become simpler
Add or update backend tests for trace contract output and frontend build if touched
Run the relevant backend unittest modules and frontend build if touched

userStories.title: Harden Task Detail Recovery Payload
userStories.description: As a product maintainer, I want task-detail responses to expose a stable recovery payload for failure type, strategy, attempt count, and resume origin so the UI can explain recovery decisions directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Task detail payloads expose complete structured recovery fields from the main runtime path
Recovery metadata no longer depends on stitching together partially related fields in the frontend
The new or clarified payload stays compatible with current task detail consumers
Add or update backend tests and frontend build if touched
Run the matching backend unittest modules and frontend build if touched

userStories.title: Add Runtime Hardening Regression Harness
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want a compact regression harness for recovery flow, context governance, tool permission context, and trace payload output so later automation rounds do not silently break the hardened runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: A focused regression test set covers the main runtime-hardening behaviors introduced across R11-R18
The recommended minimal regression command is documented in project docs or progress notes
The harness is small enough for regular use but broad enough to catch the most likely regressions
Run the new regression-oriented backend test selection and any matching frontend build if touched
Update progress knowledge if new reusable debugging or runtime patterns are discovered
